Class OutboxException

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Throwable
java.lang.Exception
java.lang.RuntimeException
io.github.lobofoltran.outbox.OutboxException
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
Direct Known Subclasses:
OutboxConfigurationException, OutboxDataException, OutboxIntegrityException, OutboxTransientException, OutboxValidationException
public sealed class OutboxException extends RuntimeException permits OutboxTransientException, OutboxIntegrityException, OutboxDataException, OutboxConfigurationException, OutboxValidationException
Thrown when the outbox store rejects a write that the caller had no way to prevent — typically an underlying SQL error, a connectivity failure, or a schema mismatch.

This is an unchecked exception by design: callers are not expected to recover from outbox failures programmatically. A failure here usually means the surrounding transaction should abort.

This type is the root of a sealed hierarchy. Implementations (notably outbox-jdbc) translate driver-level errors into one of the leaf subtypes so that callers and operators can reason about the failure class without parsing SQLState codes:

  • OutboxTransientException — retry the surrounding transaction
  • OutboxIntegrityException — the row already exists; treat the publish as idempotent success or skip
  • OutboxDataException — the payload or column data is invalid for the underlying store; abort, do not retry
  • OutboxConfigurationException — schema or connectivity is wrong; fail fast at startup
  • OutboxValidationException — the caller supplied an invalid argument while building an OutboxEvent (null required field, blank string, null header key/value); abort, do not retry

Code that previously caught OutboxException continues to work unchanged because every subtype is still an OutboxException. Since 0.4.0 the hierarchy is genuinely closed: the builder and the compact constructor of OutboxEvent also raise an OutboxException subtype (OutboxValidationException) instead of NullPointerException / IllegalArgumentException, so a single catch (OutboxException e) around the builder()...build() + publish(event) sequence covers every failure originating in this library.

Since:
0.1.0

      public OutboxException(String message, Throwable cause)
      Creates a new OutboxException with the given message and cause.

      message - human-readable description of the failure.
